entries.title: Suspect charged with attempted assassination of Trump at Washington dinner
entries.snippet: Investigators say the 31-year-old California man wanted to kill as many high-level officials as possible.

entries.title: US political violence generates a familiar cycle - this time it's in overdrive
entries.snippet: In modern America, it seems violence of this kind has become an ever-present storm that can strike anywhere and at any moment.

entries.title: Jimmy Kimmel rejects White House criticism over Melania widow joke
entries.snippet: In a parody aired days before the White House Correspondents' Dinner, Kimmel called Melania an "expectant widow".

entries.title: Russian superyacht sails through Strait of Hormuz despite blockade
entries.snippet: The 141m-long vessel, linked to a close ally of Russian President Vladimir Putin, cleared the waterway despite an ongoing blockade.

entries.title: Man admits plotting attack on Taylor Swift concert in Vienna
entries.snippet: The state prosecutor told the court that police found an almost completed bomb in a search of the man's house.

entries.title: Rescuers race to free survivors trapped inside train after fatal Indonesia crash
entries.snippet: Two trains collided on the outskirts of the Indonesian capital on Monday, killing at least 15 people.

entries.title: IS says it was behind deadly Nigeria attack on football pitch
entries.snippet: Authorities say the militants raided a football pitch where people had been gathering and opened fire at random.

entries.title: Venice opera house drops incoming music director after nepotism remarks
entries.snippet: Beatrice Venezi's appointment had been marred in controversy since being announced last September, prompting orchestra members to go on strike.

entries.title: Pakistan accused of attacking Afghan university
entries.snippet: Air attacks on the province of Kunar have killed at least seven people and injured 75, sources tell the BBC.

entries.title: Colombia offers record $1.4m-reward for rebel it blames for deadly bomb attack
entries.snippet: Officials say a rebel leader known as "Marlon" ordered the attack in which 20 people were killed.

entries.title: Russian fighters confirm withdrawal from northern Mali city after separatist attacks
entries.snippet: Ethnic Tuareg fighters say they are in control of Kidal following a weekend of nationwide attacks, alongside Islamist groups.

entries.title: Gunmen raid Nigerian orphanage and kidnap children
entries.snippet: The attack saw 23 children and the facility's proprietress kidnapped by gunmen on Sunday.

entries.title: Michael Jackson biopic smashes box office record
entries.snippet: The film received poor reviews but has stormed the box office, taking $217m (£160m) globally.

entries.title: At least 42 killed in Chad after water well dispute escalates
entries.snippet: The dispute was initially between two families before escalating into a cycle of reprisal attacks.

entries.title: Kim Jong Un opens memorial for N Korean soldiers killed in Ukraine war
entries.snippet: Kim Jong Un and Russian Defence Minister Andrey Belousov unveiled the memorial in Pyongyang.

entries.title: French coastguard rescues more than 100 migrants crossing Channel
entries.snippet: A boat carrying 106 people broke down at sea, while others were rescued attempting to board the vessel.

entries.title: Plane crash in South Sudan kills all 14 on board
entries.snippet: The authorities are investigating but early reports suggest bad weather and poor visibility may be to blame.

entries.title: 'It's bizarre': Californians grapple with revelation that suspected Trump gunman suspect was neighbour
entries.snippet: People living near the man charged with the attempted assassination of Trump say they were shocked to see their neighbour on TV.

entries.title: Performer describes locking eyes with Trump as they ducked for cover during shooting
entries.snippet: Oz Pearlman - a self-described "mentalist" - was speaking to Trump, First Lady Melania Trump and press secretary Karoline Leavitt as the attack happened.

entries.title: Ukraine's drone commander has Russian oil, troops and morale in his sights
entries.snippet: In a rare interview, Commander Robert Brovdi shared how his unit accounts for a third of all targets destroyed on the battlefield.

entries.title: WHCA dinner shooter charged. And, Charles III to address Congress
entries.snippet: Cole Allen, the man who tried to storm the White House Correspondents' Dinner, is being charged with trying to assassinate President Trump. And, King Charles III is set to address Congress today.

entries.title: Deadlock over Iran's nuclear program and the Strait of Hormuz cripples peace efforts
entries.snippet: Two months after the U.S.-Israeli attack on Iran started the war, peace talks are on hold, with control of the Strait of Hormuz and the future of Iran's nuclear program as the two main points of contention.

entries.title: The MAHA movement is mad about glyphosate and Trump's EPA
entries.snippet: Some people in the MAHA movement are angry with the Trump administration's stance on environmental toxins — including its current support for the maker of the pesticide glyphosate.

entries.title: 'We don't know what will happen to us': U.S. deportees in limbo in DRC
entries.snippet: 15 South American migrants and asylum seekers deported from the U.S. to the DRC are now living in uncertainty in a country an with ongoing armed conflict, where they have no ties.

entries.title: Millions of homes in the U.S. are uninsured. NPR wants to hear your story
entries.snippet: Millions of homes in the U.S. are uninsured, partly because insurance costs have soared in recent years. NPR wants to hear about the coverage decisions you're making as premiums rise.

entries.title: Lawsuits accuse State Farm of secretly working to cut insurance payouts
entries.snippet: Lawsuits allege that State Farm tries to avoid paying what it owes for hail damage. The litigation is happening as homeowners face soaring insurance costs, partly due to threats from climate change.

entries.title: Should schools get rid of homework? Some educators are saying yes
entries.snippet: Some experts worry that less homework could be a problem for math achievement, at a time when test scores nationwide are already at a dismal low.

entries.title: Stuck in limbo: millions of professionals risk losing legal status under Trump pause
entries.snippet: Their experiences — of sudden financial insecurity, months of unemployment, and crippling anxiety — come as the administration seeks to restrict legal migration and boost mass deportation.

entries.title: So far, Florida has failed to end vaccine mandates. Now there's a last-ditch effort
entries.snippet: Florida Republicans have pushed hard to drop some vaccine requirements for children. But after many months, their efforts stalled out. Could this week's special legislative session get the job done?

entries.title: Trump's embrace of King Charles comes at a fraught time for U.S.-UK relations
entries.snippet: Trump seems to be looking forward to hosting, in recent weeks bringing up the royal visit multiple times.

entries.title: Accused Correspondents' Dinner gunman charged with attempted assassination of Trump
entries.snippet: The suspect who attempted to storm a press gala this weekend in Washington has been charged with the attempted assassination of President Trump and other federal weapons charges. If convicted, he could face up to life in prison. White House correspondent Liz Landers was at the di

entries.title: Liz Landers and Lisa Desjardins describe chaos at Correspondents' Dinner shooting
entries.snippet: White House correspondent Liz Landers and Congressional correspondent Lisa Desjardins were at the White House Correspondents' Dinner and joined Geoff Bennett to discuss the chaos in the room that night.

entries.title: With new student loan changes, borrowers fear unsustainable payments. Experts fear a default crisis
entries.snippet: If the U.S. isn't already in the midst of a student loan delinquency and default crisis, we may be rapidly approaching one, experts told PBS News.

entries.title: Ex-agent weighs in on Secret Service security concerns
entries.snippet: To discuss the security questions surrounding Saturday's assassination attempt, Amna Nawaz spoke with Bill Gage. He was a Secret Service special agent for 12 years, including serving as a counter-assault team leader, and is currently the executive protection director for the Safe

entries.title: Fact-checking misinformation about the Correspondents' Dinner shooting
entries.snippet: Shots fired inside the hotel hosting the White House Correspondents' Association dinner attended by President Donald Trump caused immediate confusion about the suspect and what transpired at the April 25 event.

entries.title: U.S.-Iran talks at impasse over nuclear program and Strait of Hormuz
entries.snippet: The U.S. said it's discussing a new Iranian proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz but pause all negotiations over Iran's nuclear program. Meanwhile, Lebanon marked the deadliest day since the fraying ceasefire in that country went into effect as Israeli attacks killed more than

entries.title: News Wrap: King Charles arrives in Washington for state visit
entries.snippet: In our news wrap Monday, King Charles arrived at the White House as he kicked off a state visit marking the 250th anniversary of America's independence from Britain, the fight over redistricting gained steam with Florida Gov. DeSantis unveiling a map that could help Republicans a

entries.title: A look at health concerns as Roundup case reaches Supreme Court
entries.snippet: The Supreme Court heard arguments in a case that could reshape a years-long legal fight over Roundup, the most widely used weedkiller. The chemical has been a critical tool for industrial agriculture across the world, but thousands of lawsuits allege a key ingredient is linked to

entries.title: Tamara Keith and Amy Walter on the growing risks of political violence
entries.snippet: NPR's Tamara Keith and Amy Walter of the Cook Political Report with Amy Walter join Amna Nawaz to discuss the latest political news, including the fallout from the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting and another state pushes for mid-decade redistricting.

entries.title: New focus on autism fuels debate over splitting the spectrum
entries.snippet: Last year, Health Secretary Kennedy thrust autism into the national spotlight, calling it an epidemic and vowing to investigate what he characterized as its environmental causes. It struck a nerve in the autism community and reignited debates about whether the autism spectrum is

entries.title: How conspiracy theories spread after the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting
entries.snippet: There was a steady stream of facts from myriad reputable media outlets — hardly an information vacuum. Despite this, unfounded conspiracy theories from both the left and the right proliferated, chief among them that the shooting was staged.

entries.title: After legal switchbacks, one of Harvey Weinstein's accusers testifies for a third time
entries.snippet: Jessica Mann once had reason to think she was done being publicly grilled about Harvey Weinstein.

entries.title: Trumps call for ABC to fire Jimmy Kimmel — again — after morbid joke about first lady
entries.snippet: The remark about the president's wife was part of a routine on Thursday's "Jimmy Kimmel Live" where the host pretended to deliver a comedy routine at the White House Correspondents' Association dinner. That event two nights later was cut short when a man armed with guns and knive

entries.title: WATCH: Blanche, Patel and Pirro announce federal charges against accused Trump dinner gunman
entries.snippet: The man who authorities say tried to storm the White House Correspondents' Association dinner with guns and knives was charged Monday with the attempted assassination of President Donald Trump as federal authorities suggested an attack that disrupted one of Washington's glitziest

entries.title: Haitians and Syrians aren't only immigrants watching Supreme Court arguments on Temporary Protected Status
entries.snippet: Many Salvadorans have lived in the United States for 25 years under Temporary Protected Status, which allows those already in the country to stay with work permits.

entries.title: Trump fires independent board overseeing National Science Foundation
entries.snippet: Members of the National Science Board received an email on Friday sent from the Presidential Personnel Office stating that their position was terminated.

entries.title: Virginia Supreme Court considers GOP challenge to voter-approved redistricting plan favoring Democrats
entries.snippet: Virginia's Supreme Court is considering whether a voter-approved redistricting amendment complied with the state's constitutional requirements.

entries.title: California oil fight tests state's right to push back against Washington during war
entries.snippet: California continues to fight the Trump administration after it defied state officials to restart oil drilling off the Santa Barbara coast in the name of national security.

entries.title: United CEO claims merger with American Airlines would be good for travelers
entries.snippet: Scott Kirby outlined why he thinks a merger between his airline and rival American would benefit travelers, despite American's refusal to engage in negotiations.

entries.title: Preservationists won't drop lawsuit against Trump's $400M White House ballroom after DOJ request
entries.snippet: The Department of Justice requested the lawsuit be withdrawn after a shooting at the White House Correspondents' Dinner on Saturday.

entries.title: DW
entries.snippet: News, Analysis and Service from Germany and Europe - in 30 Languages

entries.title: Japan looks to woo allies with new weapons deals
entries.snippet: Tokyo has scrapped a decades-old ban on exporting lethal weapons, aiming to bolster allies as security tensions with China and North Korea intensify.

entries.title: Greek police hunt for alleged 89-year-old shooter on the run
entries.snippet: Greek police are looking for a gunman, reportedly aged 89, who opened fire on a social security office and a courthouse in central Athens, wounding several, local media reports say.

entries.title: South Korea: Ex-first lady Kim handed longer graft sentence
entries.snippet: Kim Keon Hee, wife of Yoon Suk Yeol, now faces a four-year jail sentence on charges of stock manipulation and bribery. The court also found she accepted gifts from a contentious church, knowing it expected favors.

entries.title: Austrian man admits plot to attack 2024 Taylor Swift concert
entries.snippet: An Austrian citizen accused of planning an attack at one of Taylor Swift's concerts in Vienna in 2024 pleaded guilty as his trial opened on Tuesday. The man was also tried on various terrorism charges.

entries.title: Fact check: Cloud seeding didn't make it rain in Iran
entries.snippet: Some social media users have claimed the alleged destruction of a climate research center in the United Arab Emirates caused intense precipitation in Iran. But those claims are misleading.

entries.title: Bundesliga games abroad unlikely, but for how long?
entries.snippet: New proposals from FIFA will allow domestic leagues to host one game abroad a season. As a result, Europe's football leagues could face a big change sooner rather than later.

entries.title: What Renault's new EV reveals about the global auto industry
entries.snippet: French carmaker Renault no longer sells its brand in the Chinese market, but it's not afraid to design and develop them there. It's just one more sign of big shifts in the global auto industry.

entries.title: Iran war: US wary of peace plan postponing nuclear deal
entries.snippet: The White House has reportedly not ruled out a proposal from Iran to end its chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz if the US lifts a blockade. However, US officials say any agreement must include a nuclear deal. DW has more.

entries.title: Jimmy Kimmel says Melania Trump joke not 'call to assassination'
entries.snippet: Donald and Melania Trump attacked the comedian for a joke he made several days before a man opened fire at the White House Correspondents' Dinner.

entries.title: India news: Google starts work on largest AI hub outside US in Vizag
entries.snippet: Work has begun on a sprawling $15 billion AI hub in Visakhapatnam as India pushes to become a tech powerhouse. Meanwhile, West Bengal is getting ready for a second round of voting. DW has the latest.

entries.title: Canada's Arctic noise is no party for narwhals
entries.snippet: Known as the "unicorns of the sea," narwhals are fleeing Canada's far north. Researchers suspect a link to noise pollution from increasing ship traffic.

entries.title: Mexico: Special forces capture cartel leader 'El Jardinero'
entries.snippet: A leading member of the Jalisco cartel and possible successor to "El Mencho," who was killed earlier this year, was nabbed by the Mexican military while hiding in a ditch.

entries.title: Germany news: Major raids carried out against Hells Angels
entries.snippet: A large-scale police operation in the western state of North Rhine-Westphalia has targeted biker gang crime. Meanwhile, Germany is to bid again for a non-permanent seat in the UN Security Council. DW has more.

entries.title: Indonesia: Many killed in train collision near Jakarta
entries.snippet: A long-distance train crashed into the rear car of a stopped commuter train outside Jakarta, killing at least 14 people.

entries.title: Mali on edge as insurgency tests junta's resolve
entries.snippet: The death of Malian Defense Minister Sadio Camara amid a series of coordinated assaults on several Malian cities is posing a serious challenge to the junta in Bamako, analysts say.

entries.title: China blocks Meta's $2B acquisition of AI startup Manus
entries.snippet: Manus claimed to revolutionize agentic AI. Now Chinese authorities are forcing Meta to unwind its purchase of the company in what analysts believe could set a new precedent.

entries.title: Gala shooting suspect charged with attempt to murder Trump
entries.snippet: A suspect in the shooting at the White House Correspondents' Dinner made his first court appearance. Meanwhile, Britain's King Charles III arrived in the US after a security review cleared the planned trip.

entries.title: Clashes over water resources in Chad kill over 40 people
entries.snippet: Community clashes over water resources are becoming increasingly common in Chad, feuled by extreme weather. The latest violence spread across a wide area around a water well, prompting the military to intervene.

entries.title: EU weighs giving US data for fewer travel restrictions
entries.snippet: The European Union is negotiating a framework that could allow US authorities to search national databases across much of the bloc as the price for keeping visa-free travel.
